mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding labor income which feeds into consumption and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 0.25% signals the onset of a monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and suppresses S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25%, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is stagnant while consumer prices rise, supply-chain constraints erode corporate profit margins and depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and INDPRO does not significantly predict negative 20-day returns (p > 0.05) in a joint regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales predict positive S&P 500 returns, and this predictive power is concentrated in regimes where the labor market is slack, because demand resilience reprices recession risk.
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE exceeds 6%, months with rising RSAFS fail to generate positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, leading to positive earnings surprises and near-term equity appreciation.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO are not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts signals durable demand strength in interest-rate sensitive sectors, leading to positive equity returns via earnings expectations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not positively correlated with month-over-month increases in HOUST (Spearman correlation < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 2.5% year-over-year shifts the Fed reaction function toward tighter policy, depressing S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5%, 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between falling unemployment and rising payrolls creates a stronger signal of labor market dynamism than either series alone, driving positive equity returns through consumption growth expectations.
failure_condition: A joint model containing a UNRATE x PAYEMS interaction term does not outperform a simple additive model in predicting 60-day returns (R-squared improvement < 0.01).
